months.narration: January kicked off with CES 2026, where every device seemed to have an AI chip. Google released Gemini 3.0, and TypeScript 6.0 entered beta with decorator improvements.
February saw Apple announce Vision Pro 2 with a focus on developer tools. Kubernetes 1.32 went GA, and Python 3.14 hit release candidate status.
March was massive. Anthropic dropped Claude 4 with its million-token context window. GitHub launched autonomous Copilot agents, and the Rust Foundation announced the 2.0 roadmap.
April brought early Build 2026 previews from Microsoft, a major Docker acquisition in the security space, and React 20 with server components as the default.
May was dominated by Google I/O and Project Astra. OpenAI countered with GPT-5, and Deno 3.0 reached stable with full Node compatibility.
June's WWDC introduced AI-native features in Swift. The WebAssembly component model was finalized, and Linux kernel 7.0 shipped with major performance improvements.
July brought AWS security innovations at re:Inforce, Terraform 2.0 beta with a new state management engine, and an open-source LLM finally beating GPT-4 on benchmarks.

highlights: AI coding assistants hit 80% developer adoption
months.narration: August saw GitHub Universe previews, PostgreSQL 18 with AI vector indexing built-in, and surveys showing 80 percent of developers now use AI coding assistants daily.
September's iPhone 18 featured powerful on-device AI. Kubernetes Ambient Mesh went GA, and Bun 2.0 was released with Node.js-breaking performance.
October's KubeCon showcased platform engineering maturity. OpenTelemetry profiling hit 1.0, and Anthropic released a smaller, faster Claude 4 Mini.
November was dominated by re:Invent 2026 with serverless AI inference. The AI agent framework landscape consolidated around three major players, and WebGPU reached all major browsers.
December wrapped up with developer surveys confirming the AI transformation. Open-source models closed the gap with proprietary ones, and software supply chain security became a standard practice.

predictions: AI agents will handle 30% of routine coding tasks autonomously
WebAssembly will become the default for serverless edge functions
Kubernetes complexity will be fully abstracted by platform engineering tools
Open-source LLMs will match proprietary models for most tasks
Developer productivity will increase 2x with AI-augmented workflows
